turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Hey B, good sprint retrospective today? We crushed that feature rollout, right? I mean, the client loved it.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Uh, yeah, sorta. But man, those bugs at the end... We spent half the sprint firefighting. What'd you think?

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Totally agree on the bugs. That's why I pushed so hard for more QA time upfront. Remember? I said from day one we needed dedicated testing cycles instead of squeezing it in.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Wait, you did? I thought you were all about 'move fast and break things.' Like, last retro you were complaining QA slowed us down.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Nah, I mean... yeah, I get why it felt that way, but I was talking about inefficient QA, not cutting it out. Anyway, this time it bit us hard.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Haha, okay. Tangent: did you see the pizza they brought? Way better than last time's cardboard. Speaking of which, priorities – should we talk about overloading the devs next?

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Pizza was solid, yeah. Devs were slammed, no doubt. But honestly, if we'd stuck to my plan of pairing more on critical paths, we wouldn't have had those merge conflicts.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Your plan? Dude, two sprints ago you vetoed pairing because it 'wasted time on simple tasks.' Now it's the cure-all?

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Heh, well, simple tasks vs. critical paths are different beasts. Look, hindsight's 20/20, but moving forward, pairing on high-risk stuff makes sense.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Fair, but it's funny how your tune changes. Anyway, what about the backlog grooming? We keep adding fluff tickets.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Backlog was a mess. I kept saying we should trim it ruthlessly – no pet projects. That's why we overcommitted.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: You... said that? Last planning you were the one championing that 'fun side quest' ticket for the gamification thing. Said it'd boost morale.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Oh, right, that one. Yeah, but it ballooned. Point is, we need stricter prioritization now. Like, MVP only.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Classic. So, action items? More QA, more pairing, ruthless backlog?

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Yep, nail those and we're golden. Let's log it – I'll own the QA schedule tweak.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Cool, sounds good. Retro adjourned? Beers later?

input.objective: Sprint retrospective on feature rollout bugs and process improvements
